# Questions in Livestream 187

Live Stream #187: Post-Conference Apocalypse

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/NZiidCizBEQ?t=742" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">12:22</a> | How can someone lead a Bible study as an actual study rather than a roundtable discussion? | He recommended real preparation by the leader and working through the text closely, verse by verse or in small passages. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/NZiidCizBEQ?t=1277" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">21:17</a> | Was divorce easy in ancient Egypt, especially when children were involved? | He said divorce itself was normal enough, but once children were involved the hard part became dividing property and deciding who retained the house. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/NZiidCizBEQ?t=1493" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">24:53</a> | Can Luke 23:34 be translated from Greek as "Father, forgive them not, for they know what they do"? | He said no; the passage means "forgive them, for they do not know what they are doing," not the opposite. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/NZiidCizBEQ?t=1936" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">32:16</a> | How should Christians answer an atheist who says worshiping Jesus means approving human sacrifice? | He called that a red herring, because Christians do not approve human sacrifice; Christ's self-giving is tied to God's mercy and grace. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/NZiidCizBEQ?t=2102" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">35:02</a> | Why do people say the Sphinx is 12,000 years old? | He traced that idea to the Sphinx erosion theory and emphasized that its main promoters were not trained Egyptologists and often had mystical or occult interests. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/NZiidCizBEQ?t=2725" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">45:25</a> | In Exodus 7:11, what are the "secret arts"? | He explained the term as religious secrets or mysteries, implying Egyptian magical or ritual knowledge rather than ordinary stage tricks. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/NZiidCizBEQ?t=2969" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">49:29</a> | What percentage of magic could come from demonic spirits? | He said that cannot be quantified; some magic could be demonic, while other cases may be pharmacology, wishful thinking, or mistaken correlation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/NZiidCizBEQ?t=3360" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">56:00</a> | Were Egyptian slaves circumcised? | He said not typically, though some may have been, especially in higher-status households; the evidence is limited. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/NZiidCizBEQ?t=4248" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:10:48</a> | Does the Bible actually state that magic is real, and are there biblical occult texts? | He said the Bible often reflects ancient assumptions about magic without straightforwardly affirming them, so he would not call any occult text canonical or pseudocanonical on that basis. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/NZiidCizBEQ?t=4566" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:16:06</a> | In Genesis 3:5, is the serpent linking death with being the opposite of, or closer to, God? | He said the serpent is denying God's warning and holding out the promise of becoming like God in knowing good and evil. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/NZiidCizBEQ?t=5013" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:23:33</a> | What do you make of the Megiddo Mosaic? | He saw it as a strong early witness that Christians were already identifying Jesus as God well before Nicaea. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/NZiidCizBEQ?t=5125" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:25:25</a> | Why do skeptics expect the Bible to include modern science such as the Big Bang, dinosaurs, evolution, and a spherical world? | He called that an immature, presentist way of reading ancient texts, expecting them to speak in modern categories. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/NZiidCizBEQ?t=5463" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:31:03</a> | Other than AB, what scholars should be avoided? | He cautioned especially about scholars influenced by David Rohl's chronology work, while explaining the concern as methodological rather than personal. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/NZiidCizBEQ?t=5887" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:38:07</a> | Is Got Questions another source to be cautious about? | He said he was not sure, but named Catholic Answers as one source he is cautious about. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/NZiidCizBEQ?t=5950" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:39:10</a> | Could a member of the Trinity do ungodly acts by putting aside attributes, as in Philippians 2:7? | He said no; "emptying himself" does not mean giving up divine holiness or becoming capable of evil. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/NZiidCizBEQ?t=6162" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:42:42</a> | What would angels have looked like to humans in the early New Testament? | He said the passages present encounters with spiritual beings, often perceived in humanlike or fearsome ways, rather than giving a fixed physical description. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/NZiidCizBEQ?t=6813" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:53:33</a> | If angel accounts vary greatly, how can they be factual physical-witness accounts? | He said they are not simple physical-witness accounts; human perception fills in encounters with spiritual beings. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/NZiidCizBEQ?t=7059" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:57:39</a> | In a debate with an LDS person, what resources help distinguish Mormon beliefs from Christian beliefs? | He said older resources like Walter Martin's material were once useful, but Mormon theology has shifted, so modern sources need caution. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/NZiidCizBEQ?t=8403" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:20:03</a> | Is the young-earth position likely to go the way of flat-earth claims, and were Christians never flat-earthers until recently? | He said round-earth knowledge is very old, and claims about a universally flat ancient Christian worldview are wrong. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/NZiidCizBEQ?t=8814" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:26:54</a> | Is there evidence against the claim that Nicaea excluded a large group of non-orthodox or non-Trinitarian believers? | He said Mormon-style claims confuse terms: the Trinity is orthodox monotheism, and hardline Arian bishops at Nicaea were few. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/NZiidCizBEQ?t=9212" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:33:32</a> | Were there Christian belief groups that could not attend the Council of Nicaea? | He said the council focused on one main issue, and groups such as modalists and gnostics had already been excluded from orthodox Christianity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/NZiidCizBEQ?t=9937" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:45:37</a> | What view of theistic evolution do you hold? | He said he does not hold an origins position and regards concordist approaches, including theistic evolution, as flawed. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/NZiidCizBEQ?t=10341" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:52:21</a> | Was the area of Sodom more fertile in Abraham's time? | He said yes; the Middle Bronze Age setting fits a period of greater rainfall and better-watered land. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/NZiidCizBEQ?t=10595" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:56:35</a> | What are your thoughts on Jephthah's 300-year defense? | He rejected that defense as problematic, saying Jephthah's letter contains several historical mistakes beyond the 300-year figure. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/NZiidCizBEQ?t=10991" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:03:11</a> | Is anyone who believes in a rapture of the elect a false prophet? | He said the text points to the wicked being taken, but eschatology has disputed views and prophetic claims should be treated cautiously. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/NZiidCizBEQ?t=11849" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:17:29</a> | What do you think of Walton's book on prophets? | He generally recommends Walton's work, but had not read that specific book and could not give a firm opinion. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/NZiidCizBEQ?t=11911" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:18:31</a> | Did ancient Egyptians travel to Ireland? | He said no, not ancient Egyptians, though modern Egyptians may have. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/NZiidCizBEQ?t=12127" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:22:07</a> | Should Christians pray individually to the Father, Son, and Holy Spirit? | He said it is permitted, not prescribed; Christians may pray to any person of the Trinity or to the triune God. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/NZiidCizBEQ?t=12421" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:27:01</a> | Was Nun half Egyptian, and was he a priest in an Egyptian temple because of his name? | He said both are possible but uncertain, since many Israelites had Egyptian or Canaanite religious contact in Egypt. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/NZiidCizBEQ?t=12649" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:30:49</a> | Who is the shortest man in the Bible? | He said the Bible notes Zacchaeus was short, but does not clearly identify a shortest man. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/NZiidCizBEQ?t=14232" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:57:12</a> | Would Old Testament authors be able to read modern Hebrew writing? | He said later biblical authors using Aramaic script would be much closer to modern written Hebrew, though changes in script and later meanings still matter. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/NZiidCizBEQ?t=14581" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:03:01</a> | Are 600 chariots pursuing the Hebrews in an Exodus a reasonable number? | He said yes, because the text refers to elite chariots, and the figure fits what is known about Ramesside armies. |
